ordinary|two separate blockers now clear. **google** code path is correct now: `expo-web-browser.openauthsessionasync` = ios `aswebauthenticationsession`, android chrome custom tab. old installed app cannot have `expowebbrowser`; native modules require rebuild. i started new ios dev build: [build ce94db83](https://expo.dev/accounts/jeppygeja/projects/vex-app/builds/ce94db83-45a6-45cb-bac9-8d487e0e995c) status when checked: `in progress`. **apple** native apple worked. fingerprint showed. failure is supabase config: `unacceptable audience in id_token: [com.jonathan.vex]` that means apple token `aud` is your ios bundle id, but supabase apple provider does not accept `com.jonathan.vex`. fix in supabase dashboard: `authentication -> providers -> apple -> client ids` set it to include: ```text com.jonathan.vex ``` if you also use web apple oauth, comma-list both: ```text com.jonathan.vex,your.services.id ``` docs confirm this: supabase apple provider client ids must include native bundle ids for native ios, and google/apple multi-client setups use comma-separated client ids. sources: [supabase apple auth](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/social-login/auth-apple), [supabase google auth](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/social-login/auth-google), [supabase expo social auth](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/quickstarts/with-expo-react-native-social-auth). verified code: - removed `expo-crypto` dependency path causing `expocrypto` fatal. - kept google system auth session. - `npm test -- --runtestsbypath src/features/auth/__tests__/auth-oauth-service.test.ts --runinband` passes. - `npx tsc --noemit` passes.

ordinary|done. changed: - merged `plan` into `progress` as internal `overview / plan` subnavigation. - removed `plan` from bottom tabs, route types, tab icon mapping, and main navigator. - redirected home “plan” entry points to `progress`. - fixed “could not load today’s plan” root cause: supabase returns `snake_case`, ui schemas expect `camelcase`; repository now maps through zod parse boundary. - replaced placeholder week view with real week task data. - removed dead plan action stubs; project/study ctas now route somewhere real. - tightened plan ui to match vex glass language better: segmented pills, glass cards, compact error states, proper a11y labels/touch targets. - kept all touched files under 200 lines. key files: - [progressscreen.tsx](c:/users/jonat/cascadeprojects/vex-app-old/src/screens/progress/progressscreen.tsx) - [planworkspace.tsx](c:/users/jonat/cascadeprojects/vex-app-old/src/screens/plan/components/planworkspace.tsx) - [repository-mappers.ts](c:/users/jonat/cascadeprojects/vex-app-old/src/features/plan/repository-mappers.ts) - [mainnavigator.tsx](c:/users/jonat/cascadeprojects/vex-app-old/src/navigation/mainnavigator.tsx) verified: - `rtk npx tsc --noemit` passes. - targeted jest passes: `route-param-schemas`, `route-registration-audit`, `session-start-navigation-schemas-deep`. note: repo already had many dirty/untracked files before/around this task, including untracked `src/features/plan/` and `src/screens/plan/`; i did not revert unrelated work.

ordinary|**read** screenshot problem clear: current `vex path` is giant vertical roadmap. too much copy, cards, timeline, badges, scroll. home becomes feature museum. web check: 2026 mobile ux direction = progressive disclosure, adaptive/personalized ui, minimal surfaces, micro-interactions, ethical transparency, performance-first. sources align: [ixdf progressive disclosure](https://ixdf.org/literature/topics/progressive-disclosure), [muzli 2026 mobile patterns](https://muz.li/blog/whats-changing-in-mobile-design-ui-patterns-that-matter-in-2026/), [dignexus 2026 ux trends](https://www.dignexus.com/blog/ui-ux-trends-in-mobile-apps-2026-a-strategic-guide-for-startups-smes-enterprises/). x/twitter search not reliable enough from web index; use broader current design sources. **best direction** replace long path with one compact module: `next unlocks` single home card, max 220-260px tall. shows only: 1. next feature user is about to unlock 2. one teaser after that everything else hidden. no full roadmap on home. layout: ```text next unlocks [active unlock card] reveal your baseline finish 1 session progress bar: 0 / 1 primary cta: start focus [small locked teaser] after that: find your first rhythm needs 2 finishes ``` no points. say “finish sessions” / “complete focused work” / “use vex enough to learn pattern.” points feel gamey and cheap. **visual shape** use bento-ish compact panel, not timeline. - left: tiny glowing “signal core” icon / orb - middle: feature name + human explanation - bottom: slim progress rail - right/top: `next` pill - second unlock = collapsed row, frosted/locked, smaller example copy: ```text vex is learning your baseline. finish one focus session to unlock your first recap. next: rhythm detection after two finishes, vex can compare how you start. ``` **interaction** tap module opens bottom sheet, not new giant screen. bottom sheet: - “why this unlocks” - “what vex has seen” - “what to do next” - “feature stays hidden until ready” this uses pasted philosophy: evidence-based, lane-aware, non-manipulative, hidden features inert. **current 2 unlocks** for day 0 / new user: 1. `session_recap` / “reveal your baseline” - unlock: 1 completed session - shows: first finish recap, not advanced stats 2. `rhythm_detection` / “find your first rhythm” - unlock: 2 completed sessions - shows: start-time / completion pattern comparison better names maybe: - `first signal` - `rhythm lock` - `vex memory` **3 advanced 2026 features to add** keep advanced, adaptive, useful. no economy, no social, no fake game clutter. 1. `adaptive_home` - vex rearranges home based on what user actually uses. - unlock: enough behavior signal, maybe score 3 or 3 sessions. - user-facing: “vex cleans your home around your rhythm.” 2. `intent_brief` - before session, vex creates 1-line mission from project + prior failures. - unlock: after repeated project/focus usage. - user-facing: “start with the right next move, not blank pressure.” 3. `recovery_mode` - if user misses rhythm or quits sessions early, vex offers smaller fallback session. - unlock: after app has enough drop-off evidence. - user-facing: “when momentum drops, vex shrinks the task instead of shaming you.” **my pick** home should show: - compact `next unlocks` card - only 2 features max - no timeline - no full scroll path - no points - bottom sheet for explanation - advanced features hidden until relevant this makes unlocks feel like product intelligence, not mobile game checklist.

ordinary|**short answer** yes. day 0 should not be “do focus session or nothing.” current app already has pieces for better day 0: - plan - coach - capture / brain dump / voice / photo / link - companion - today strip - study layer - rescue cta but current day 0 still *feels* focus-only because unlock policy blocks richer surfaces and home visual hierarchy over-promotes first session. **mode renames** current names bad: - `minimal_normal` - `deep_creative` - `game_like` - `student` - `run-clean` / `project` style labels better user-facing names: - `minimal_normal` → **clean mode** - “keep today simple. one useful action at a time.” - `deep_creative` → **build mode** - “projects, ideas, drafts, long creative arcs.” - `game_like` → **quest mode** - “challenges, bosses, streak combat, fast feedback.” - `student` → **study mode** - “study plans, reviews, quizzes, learning memory.” top picker names: - “project mode” → **build mode** - “run-clean” → **clean mode** - “game-like” → **quest mode** - “student” → **study mode** vex voice: `clean / build / quest / study` simple. branded. understandable. **what code says** current day 0 permitted surfaces: - `start_session` - `coach_presence` - `unlock_strip` - `study_layer` - `boss_teaser` - `companion_thread` - `rescue_cta` - `today_strip` blocked on day 0: - `project_thread` - `run_board` - `challenge_teaser` - `weekly_quest` - `memory_insight` - `focus_window` - `study_os` - `weekly_intelligence` - `boss_compact` also `homedayzerolaunchpad` already has cards for: - focus - plan - coach - baseline - coach again so issue is not zero code. issue is day 0 lacks **real alternate loops**. **2026 research takeaway** current product trend: ai-native apps should create action, not show dashboards. relevant signals: - google stitch 2026 pushes prompt/voice-to-interface, multi-screen journey generation, ai-native canvas, and design agent memory. meaning: users expect “tell app what i want” then app shapes flow. source: [google stitch](https://stitch.withgoogle.com/), [google blog](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-labs/stitch-ai-ui-design/). - productivity apps in 2026 lean into ai scheduling, energy-aware planning, task extraction, and unified capture. source: [lifestack 2026 productivity roundup](https://lifestack.ai/blog/productivity-tools-updates-2026), [storyflow 2026 productivity apps](https://storyflow.so/blog/best-productivity-apps-2026). - finch proves pet/self-care works because users complete tiny wellness actions to grow companion, not only focus sessions. source: [finch play store](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.finch.finch&hl=en_us). - habitica/mainquest prove quests/tasks can be action layer, but modern users need less clutter, offline support, adhd-friendly ui. source: [habitica](https://habitica.com/), [mainquest vs habitica 2026](https://www.mainquest.net/mainquest-vs-habitica). - forest proves focus timer loop works, but vex should not copy only timer. source: [forest](https://forestapp.cc/). **big product decision** bring **3 non-focus actions** to day 0. not full features. lightweight “first moves.” day 0 should offer: 1. **start focus** - existing. - for users ready to work. 2. **brain dump** - use existing capture feature. - user can type/voice/photo/link what is on mind. - vex turns it into possible quest/task later. - unlock signal: `capture_created`. 3. **make tiny plan** - use existing plan/vex-actions. - user picks one thing: - “i need to study” - “i need to build” - “i need to clean up tasks” - output: 1-3 tiny actions. - unlock signal: `plan_item_created`. 4. **meet companion** - use existing companion/companion-promise. - not full pet economy. no shop. no feeding grind. - day 0 action: - name companion - choose vibe - make one promise - unlock signal: `coach_interaction` or `companion_promise_created`. this fixes day 0. **best new feature idea** not “self-care pet” exactly. vex already has companion. make it: **vex anchor** a tiny companion/system entity that asks: `what should i remember about you today?` user chooses one: - “i’m overwhelmed” - “i need to study” - “i’m building something” - “i just need a clean start” then vex creates: - one suggested action - one promise - one os unlock path - one companion memory seed this is stronger than pet because it fits vex. pet alone = cute. anchor = useful + emotional + unlock signal. **day 0 home proposal** top card: `vex os 5% online` `choose your first signal.` then 4 action tiles: - **focus block** - `start a timer. build baseline.` - **brain dump** - `drop thoughts. vex finds the next move.` - **tiny plan** - `make a 3-step path for today.` - **meet anchor** - `set your companion promise.` after one action, os progresses: - focus → unlock session recap path - brain dump → unlock memory console teaser sooner - tiny plan → unlock today strip / project thread teaser sooner - meet anchor → unlock companion detail teaser sooner **progressive unlock changes** current unlock is too session-heavy. better: - `companion_detail`: unlock by 1 companion promise or 3 sessions - `memory_console`: unlock by 2 captures or 3 sessions - `today_strip`: unlock by 1 plan item or 1 session - `project_thread`: unlock by project intent + 1 capture + 1 plan, not 5 sessions - `challenges`: unlock after user chooses quest mode or completes 2 actions - `study_intelligence`: unlock after study mode + 1 study plan/capture, not only sessions do not bring `boss_tab`, `achievements`, `premium_paywall`, or full `content_study` to day 0. too heavy. **recommended day 0 unlocks** bring forward: - `today_strip` - `companion_thread` - `capture` - `plan` - `ai_coach_basic` - `study_layer` only if study mode - `boss_teaser` only if quest mode - `project seed` only if build mode keep gated: - boss - achievements - premium - advanced coach - weekly intelligence - full study os - quiz review **final shape** day 0 should be: `choose first signal` not: `start focus session` that one wording change matters. vex is not a focus app. vex is an adaptive execution os. day 0 must prove that immediately.

ordinary|added these to eas: ```bash expo_public_supabase_anon_key expo_public_sentry_dsn expo_public_posthog_key ``` remote check now only missing: ```bash supabase_service_role_key ``` android-only missing, ignore if no android: ```bash eas_google_service_account_key_path expo_public_revenuecat_android_key ``` i cannot add `supabase_service_role_key`: not in `.env.local` or `.env.server`. get it: supabase dashboard → project settings → api → `service_role` key. then either send/add it, or run: ```bash eas secret:create --scope project --name supabase_service_role_key --value "your_service_role_key" --type string --force --non-interactive ``` then ios secrets done.

ordinary|“open design” here is not a separate design tool. it means **openai’s computer use design/desktop-control surface**: codex can open and operate windows apps visually through the `computer-use@openai-bundled` plugin. what it can do: - list/open windows apps - take screenshots of app windows - read accessibility ui text - click/type/scroll/drag/press keys - automate apps like notepad, browsers, ides, design tools, etc. what it is not: - not figma - not a design generator by itself - not “opendesign” product/app - not web browsing unless it controls a browser app name confusion likely from link label: `[@open design](plugin://computer-use@openai-bundled)`. the target says real plugin is **computer use**.

ordinary|retest says `.env.server` still has same non-unified-looking key: ```txt keylen=71 startsfreellmapi=false base=http://167.71.255.199:3001/v1 model=auto bearer=401 invalid api key x-api-key=401 invalid api key ``` so either: - you updated supabase secrets, but not local `.env.server` - `.env.server` has different key than dashboard - freellmapi unified key on your instance does not use repo format, but server still rejects it need simplest check: open `.env.server`. first chars of `llm_api_key` must be: ```txt freellmapi- ``` no quotes. no spaces. if you only updated supabase secrets, local retest will still fail. then deployed edge may use new secret, but local `.env.server` won’t. to retest deployed, need deploy functions first: ```bash npx supabase functions deploy ai-coach npx supabase functions deploy ai npx supabase functions deploy content-study ``` then test app ai coach.
